name: edwson-design-system-components
note: Each component is a CONTRACT, not just markup. The metadata (whenToUse / whenNot / props / a11y / regulatory / dataContract) is what lets an AI agent pick and wire the right component on the first try — which is where the token savings come from (fewer correction loops, no value regeneration).

OrderTicket.purpose: Place a buy/sell order with pre-trade margin + compliance checks.
OrderTicket.whenToUse: Any execution surface where a user submits an order.
OrderTicket.whenNot: Read-only blotters or historical fills — use TradeBlotter.
OrderTicket.a11y: Keyboard-first; SEND is reversible with a visible cancel window; FIX OrdStatus drives state, not animation.

RiskWarning.purpose: Mandatory, non-removable risk disclosure before a high-risk action.
RiskWarning.whenToUse: Any leveraged/CFD order entry or product page that triggers a disclosure rule.
RiskWarning.whenNot: Never optional. Cannot be visually suppressed or collapsed below the fold.

PriceTicker.purpose: Live price with direction + tick animation.
PriceTicker.whenToUse: Any surface showing a streaming instrument price.
PriceTicker.whenNot: Static end-of-day reports — use a plain figure.

KycStepper.purpose: Progressive multi-step KYC/AML onboarding with save-and-resume.
KycStepper.whenNot: Single-field edits — use a standard form.
KycStepper.a11y: One section at a time; clear progress; plain-language labels.

RegCitation.purpose: Inline legal basis shown beside an intrusive question.
RegCitation.whenNot: Decorative copy. Must reference a real rule.

AiSuggestion.purpose: An AI recommendation that requires explicit human sign-off.
AiSuggestion.whenToUse: Any AI-proposed action affecting a client portfolio/account.
AiSuggestion.whenNot: Never auto-executes. Always pairs with SignOffBar.
AiSuggestion.a11y: Confidence shown numerically + as a meter; reasoning attached.

SignOffBar.purpose: Typed human sign-off that gates execution and writes an audit record.
SignOffBar.whenToUse: Commit points for AI-assisted or high-impact actions.
SignOffBar.whenNot: Low-stakes UI. Do not stub the audit write.

ConfidenceMeter.purpose: Calibrated confidence display with a designed-abstain state.
ConfidenceMeter.whenNot: Don't fake precision; show abstain when low.
ConfidenceMeter.a11y: Numeric + visual; abstain is a first-class state.

AutonomyTier.purpose: Select an AI autonomy level (Ambient → Co-pilot → Assertive → Autonomous); raising a tier requires explicit re-consent.
AutonomyTier.whenToUse: Anywhere an agent can act with varying independence and the user must own the boundary.
AutonomyTier.whenNot: Never default to the highest tier; never let a tier change without an explicit, logged user action.
AutonomyTier.a11y: Radiogroup semantics; the active tier is announced; tier names carry meaning, not colour alone.

TokenCostMeter.purpose: Estimate the AI-token cost of a call (input/output tokens × model price) including prompt-cache savings.
TokenCostMeter.whenToUse: Surfacing the cost of an AI action before or while it runs, or budgeting an agent workflow.
TokenCostMeter.whenNot: Never present an estimate as a billed amount; always label it as an estimate.
TokenCostMeter.a11y: A live region announces the total; figures use tabular numerals to avoid reflow.

dataContract.shape: { inputTokens, outputTokens, priceIn, priceOut, total, perThousand }

SchemaContract.purpose: Define a table's columns (name/type/nullable) and emit a live CREATE TABLE DDL — the data contract engineering ships.
SchemaContract.whenToUse: Authoring a schema-on-write table or data contract collaboratively before a single row is written.
SchemaContract.whenNot: Not for schema-on-read exploratory CSVs; use type inference there.
SchemaContract.a11y: Each column is a labelled fieldset; type is a native select; remove buttons carry aria-labels.
regulatory: GDPR (data minimisation via typed / NOT NULL constraints)

LineageGraph.purpose: Source → transform → table provenance as a clickable DAG with upstream/downstream impact tracing.
LineageGraph.whenToUse: Impact analysis before a schema change, or root-cause when a downstream report goes stale.
LineageGraph.whenNot: For hundreds of nodes use a dedicated lineage catalog; this is a focused single-pipeline view.
LineageGraph.a11y: Nodes are buttons, keyboard-activatable; relationship encoded in the node border, not colour alone; selection announced.

ClusterTopology.purpose: Pooled unified-memory AI cluster topology with a human visual and a machine-readable manifest (EXO-style).
ClusterTopology.whenToUse: Planning or showing an on-prem distributed-inference cluster and which model fits in pooled memory.
ClusterTopology.whenNot: Never equate pooled unified memory with single-GPU VRAM; always label memory figures as estimates.
ClusterTopology.a11y: Node cards are labelled; the pooled total sits in a live region; the manifest is a labelled code region.

AuditTrail.purpose: Append-only, hash-chained event log for compliance evidence (sign-offs, overrides, disclosures shown).
AuditTrail.whenToUse: Any action that must be defensible later: human sign-off, AI override, KYC step, disclosure acknowledgement.
AuditTrail.whenNot: Never allow edit or delete of past entries; never omit the actor or the timestamp.
AuditTrail.a11y: The log is a labelled list; new entries are announced politely; timestamps are machine-readable.

OrderBook.purpose: Render a Level 2 bid/ask depth ladder with aggressor highlighting and microsecond time-priority ordering.
OrderBook.whenToUse: On execution and market-microstructure surfaces where depth, queue position, and time priority drive the trade decision.
OrderBook.whenNot: Not for a single top-of-book quote — use PriceTicker instead.
OrderBook.a11y: Ladder rows are a semantic table with row/column headers; bid/ask are distinguished by label and position, not colour alone.

RiskMatrix.purpose: Visualise portfolio concentration and risk exposure as a heatmap with an automatic throttle gate when limits are breached.
RiskMatrix.whenToUse: On risk-management surfaces where concentration must be seen at a glance and breaches must hard-stop new exposure.
RiskMatrix.whenNot: Not for a single position's P&L — use a position card; not for narrative risk reporting.
RiskMatrix.a11y: Heatmap cells carry text values and an explicit breach status; the throttle gate is announced via live region.

MarginPanel.purpose: Show margin, leverage and available buying power with jurisdiction-specific leverage caps applied.
MarginPanel.whenToUse: Anywhere a user takes leveraged exposure and the regulated cap must be enforced and visible.
MarginPanel.whenNot: Not for cash-only accounts with no leverage — omit the panel.
MarginPanel.a11y: Leverage cap and current ratio are read as labelled values; an over-cap state is announced, not colour-only.

TradeBlotter.purpose: Display an execution blotter keyed on FIX Tag 39 OrdStatus and Tag 150 ExecType with a CSDR settlement-penalty column.
TradeBlotter.whenToUse: On post-trade surfaces tracking order lifecycle, fills and settlement exposure.
TradeBlotter.whenNot: Not for submitting orders — use OrderTicket; not for live depth — use OrderBook.
TradeBlotter.a11y: Rendered as a sortable data table with column headers and scope; OrdStatus is text, not a colour swatch.

SanctionsScreen.purpose: Screen a party against OFAC and PEP lists and surface scored potential matches for analyst review.
SanctionsScreen.whenToUse: During onboarding or transaction monitoring wherever a counterparty must be sanctions-screened.
SanctionsScreen.whenNot: Not a substitute for full KYC identity capture — use KycStepper for that.
SanctionsScreen.a11y: Each match exposes its score and list source as text; clear/hit states announced via live region.

SuitabilityGate.purpose: Gate access to a financial product behind a FINRA 2111 suitability assessment before the product is shown.
SuitabilityGate.whenToUse: Before exposing any product whose suitability depends on the customer's profile.
SuitabilityGate.whenNot: Not for products with no suitability obligation — render the product directly.
SuitabilityGate.a11y: Gate outcome is announced; the blocking reason is text and focusable, not a colour state.

DisclosureModal.purpose: Present a jurisdiction-routed disclosure per ASIC RG 268 with a meaningful-engagement gate before continuing.
DisclosureModal.whenToUse: Whenever a regulated risk disclosure must be shown and genuine engagement evidenced before action.
DisclosureModal.whenNot: Not for incidental tooltips or marketing copy — use a plain modal or tooltip.
DisclosureModal.a11y: Focus is trapped, restored on close, and the acknowledge control is disabled until the engagement gate is met, announced via live region.

UboGraph.purpose: Render an ultimate-beneficial-owner ownership graph for AML diligence under FATF Recommendation 10.
UboGraph.whenToUse: During enhanced due diligence to trace control and ownership through layered legal entities.
UboGraph.whenNot: Not for a flat counterparty list — use a table; not for transaction flow — use a different graph.
UboGraph.a11y: Graph offers an equivalent navigable node list; ownership percentages and control status are read as text.

ReasoningChain.purpose: Show an AI's step-by-step reasoning as a challengeable chain with each step labelled estimate vs fact.
ReasoningChain.whenToUse: Wherever an AI conclusion must be auditable and the user can dispute individual steps.
ReasoningChain.whenNot: Not for hiding chain-of-thought you don't want exposed — omit rather than fabricate steps.
ReasoningChain.a11y: Steps form an ordered list; estimate/fact labels are text, and each step is individually focusable and challengeable.

GroundingIndicator.purpose: Annotate AI claims at the claim level as grounded, ungrounded, or contradicted, blocking send when any claim is contradicted.
GroundingIndicator.whenToUse: On AI output surfaces where claims must be traceable to sources before the user can act on them.
GroundingIndicator.whenNot: Not for free-form creative text where grounding does not apply.
GroundingIndicator.a11y: Each claim's grounding state is announced as text; a contradicted claim moves focus to the blocking control.

AgentTrace.purpose: Trace an agent's tool calls with arguments, results and latency, gating write actions behind an explicit approval boundary.
AgentTrace.whenToUse: Whenever an agent invokes tools and the user must inspect and approve side-effecting actions.
AgentTrace.whenNot: Not for read-only chat with no tool use.
AgentTrace.a11y: Trace is a structured list with labelled tool, args, result and latency; pending write approval is announced.

dataContract.shape: { spans[], tool, args, result, latencyMs, pendingApproval }

PromptComposer.purpose: Compose a prompt with attachable context chips, a live token budget, and injection-safe paste handling.
PromptComposer.whenToUse: Any input surface where users build prompts with context and must respect a token budget.
PromptComposer.whenNot: Not for a plain single-line search box — use a standard input.
PromptComposer.a11y: Chips are removable focusable elements; remaining token budget is announced via live region as it changes.

DisclosureLabel.purpose: Render an EU AI Act Article 50 label declaring content as AI-assisted or fully automated.
DisclosureLabel.whenToUse: On any surface presenting AI-generated or AI-assisted output that requires disclosure.
DisclosureLabel.whenNot: Not for purely human-authored content — no label is required.
DisclosureLabel.a11y: The label is a text role element conveying assisted vs automated state without relying on an icon alone.

ModelFallbackChain.purpose: Show an ordered model fallback chain and honestly flag when a reduced-quality model served the response.
ModelFallbackChain.whenToUse: Where multiple models back a single feature and the user should know which one actually answered.
ModelFallbackChain.whenNot: Not for single-model deployments with no fallback.
ModelFallbackChain.a11y: Active model and any degraded state are announced as text; chain order is a readable ordered list.

ModelCard.purpose: Present a model card with intended use, metrics and an explicit do-not-use panel.
ModelCard.whenToUse: Wherever a deployed model must document its scope, limitations and prohibited uses.
ModelCard.whenNot: Not for documenting a heuristic rule with no model — use plain docs.
ModelCard.a11y: Sections use proper heading structure; the do-not-use panel is a landmark region, not styled-only emphasis.

ConfusionMatrix.purpose: Render a confusion matrix framing false negatives as missed risk and false positives as analyst cost.
ConfusionMatrix.whenToUse: When evaluating a classifier whose error trade-offs carry distinct business consequences.
ConfusionMatrix.whenNot: Not for regression model evaluation — use a residual or error plot.
ConfusionMatrix.a11y: Matrix is a data table with axis headers; FN and FP business meanings are text labels, not colour-coded only.

DriftMonitor.purpose: Monitor feature/prediction drift using PSI bands with honest acknowledgement of label-delay uncertainty.
DriftMonitor.whenToUse: On model-monitoring surfaces tracking distribution shift over time.
DriftMonitor.whenNot: Not for one-off model evaluation — use ConfusionMatrix or ModelCard.
DriftMonitor.a11y: Drift band state is announced as text; the label-delay caveat is a visible, readable note.

ChampionChallenger.purpose: Compare a champion and challenger model and block promotion when the challenger regresses on fairness.
ChampionChallenger.whenToUse: During model promotion reviews where a fairness regression must hard-stop deployment.
ChampionChallenger.whenNot: Not for routine A/B copy tests with no model governance.
ChampionChallenger.a11y: Promotion eligibility and any fairness block are announced as text; metric deltas are labelled, not colour-only.

TokenizerEstimate.purpose: Estimate a token-count range for input text without a live tokenizer call.
TokenizerEstimate.whenToUse: When a user needs a quick token-cost estimate before sending text to a model.
TokenizerEstimate.whenNot: Not where exact billed token counts are required — use the provider's tokenizer.
TokenizerEstimate.a11y: The estimated range is announced via live region as the text changes; range bounds are text.

ContextWindowBudget.purpose: Show used versus remaining context-window tokens with an overflow warning at capacity.
ContextWindowBudget.whenToUse: On any surface where the running conversation or document can exceed the model's context window.
ContextWindowBudget.whenNot: Not for billing totals across requests — use UsagePricing.
ContextWindowBudget.a11y: Remaining budget and overflow state are announced via live region, not signalled by colour alone.

QualityProfiler.purpose: Profile each column's null-rate and distinct-count and surface a PASS or WARN data-quality verdict.
QualityProfiler.whenToUse: When inspecting a dataset's health before it feeds a model or report.
QualityProfiler.whenNot: Not for row-level record editing — use TypedQueryGrid.
QualityProfiler.a11y: Profile is a data table; PASS/WARN verdicts are text labels with scope headers, not colour-only.

TypedQueryGrid.purpose: Display query results in a grid with per-column type chips and type-aware sorting.
TypedQueryGrid.whenToUse: When showing tabular query output where column types should drive correct sort behaviour.
TypedQueryGrid.whenNot: Not for column-level statistical profiling — use QualityProfiler.
TypedQueryGrid.a11y: Grid is a data table with sortable column headers exposing aria-sort; type chips are text labels.

DeltaVersionDiff.purpose: Diff two table versions showing rows added, changed and removed using Delta Lake time-travel.
DeltaVersionDiff.whenToUse: When comparing snapshots of a versioned table to audit what changed between commits.
DeltaVersionDiff.whenNot: Not for diffing free-form text — use a text diff component.
DeltaVersionDiff.a11y: Change categories are text-labelled with counts; diff rows are a navigable table, not colour-only highlights.

ModelFitPartition.purpose: Check whether a model fits a cluster and compute a memory-weighted partition across nodes.
ModelFitPartition.whenToUse: When planning to load a large model and distribution across heterogeneous node memory is required.
ModelFitPartition.whenNot: Not for single-node inference where the model fits in one device — skip partitioning.
ModelFitPartition.a11y: Fit verdict and per-node share are read as labelled text values; an over-capacity state is announced.

DistributedInference.purpose: Run ring-based distributed inference exposed through an OpenAI/Claude/Ollama-compatible endpoint.
DistributedInference.whenToUse: When serving a model sharded across a node ring behind a standard inference API.
DistributedInference.whenNot: Not for single-process local inference — call the model directly.
DistributedInference.a11y: Streaming state, active node and tokens/sec are announced via live region as text.

UsagePricing.purpose: Meter usage-based consumption and map it to pricing tiers with a running cost total.
UsagePricing.whenToUse: On billing surfaces where charges accrue per unit of usage across tiered plans.
UsagePricing.whenNot: Not for flat-rate subscriptions with no metering — show a static plan card.
UsagePricing.a11y: Tier boundaries and running total are labelled text; the active tier is announced when it changes.

QuotaRateLimit.purpose: Provide a console for quota allocation and rate-limit configuration with live consumption readouts.
QuotaRateLimit.whenToUse: On admin surfaces where tenants' quotas and request rate limits are set and monitored.
QuotaRateLimit.whenNot: Not for end-user usage display — use UsagePricing.
QuotaRateLimit.a11y: Consumption against quota is announced via live region; near-limit state is text, not colour-only.

SsoScimConsole.purpose: Configure enterprise SSO and SCIM provisioning, including identity-provider mapping and sync status.
SsoScimConsole.whenToUse: On enterprise admin surfaces managing single sign-on and automated user provisioning.
SsoScimConsole.whenNot: Not for individual password or profile settings — use a user account form.
SsoScimConsole.a11y: Connection and last-sync status are announced as text; mapping rows are a labelled table.

CustomerHealth.purpose: Compute a customer health score from usage and engagement signals and flag churn risk.
CustomerHealth.whenToUse: On account-management surfaces surfacing at-risk customers for proactive outreach.
CustomerHealth.whenNot: Not for raw event analytics — use a metrics dashboard.
CustomerHealth.a11y: Health score and churn-risk tier are announced as labelled text, not colour-only badges.

ContrastChecker.purpose: Compute the WCAG relative-luminance contrast ratio between two colours and grade AA/AAA pass/fail.
ContrastChecker.whenToUse: When validating colour pairs against WCAG contrast thresholds during design or QA.
ContrastChecker.whenNot: Not for non-text decorative colour choices with no contrast requirement.
ContrastChecker.a11y: The computed ratio and AA/AAA verdicts are announced via live region as text, not signalled by colour alone.

FocusTrap.purpose: Trap keyboard focus within a modal region and restore focus to the trigger on close.
FocusTrap.whenToUse: Around any modal, dialog or drawer that must contain focus while open.
FocusTrap.whenNot: Not for inline non-modal content where focus should flow naturally past it.
FocusTrap.a11y: Tab and Shift+Tab cycle within the region, Escape closes, and focus returns to the triggering element on close.

LiveRegion.purpose: Announce dynamic content to screen readers via a polite or assertive ARIA live region.
LiveRegion.whenToUse: Whenever status, errors or results update without a page change and must be announced.
LiveRegion.whenNot: Not for static content already in the accessibility tree — announce only changes.
LiveRegion.a11y: Implements an aria-live region with the chosen politeness; messages are text-only with no colour dependence.

CrossPlatformWindow.purpose: Render OS-adaptive window chrome matching macOS, Windows, Linux or Ubuntu conventions.
CrossPlatformWindow.whenToUse: When emulating native window controls and layout that must feel correct per operating system.
CrossPlatformWindow.whenNot: Not for browser-engine differences — use CrossBrowserShell.
CrossPlatformWindow.a11y: Window controls expose accessible names regardless of OS styling; layout differences do not change focus order.

CrossBrowserShell.purpose: Provide engine-aware rendering via @supports feature queries with graceful fallbacks for Blink, Gecko and WebKit.
CrossBrowserShell.whenToUse: When a feature must degrade predictably across rendering engines using progressive enhancement.
CrossBrowserShell.whenNot: Not for OS-level window chrome — use CrossPlatformWindow; never UA-sniff for engine detection.
CrossBrowserShell.a11y: Both the enhanced and fallback paths expose the same semantics and focus order; engine differences are invisible to assistive tech.
